methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Baykar is a private Defense & Security company founded by Selçuk and Haluk Bayraktar. The broader Defense & Security sector contains 21 peer companies, of which 2 are publicly traded (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 are private. However, the peer set lacks sufficient granularity for traditional comparable-company analysis: only 1 same-stage private peer exists, with 0 additional companies in adjacent financing stages or matching valuation tier, falling short of the minimum 3 datapoints required for robust peer-median triangulation. The top identified private comparables include Scale AI, Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI, though none provide tight multiples anchors.

Given data constraints, peer-median methodology is inapplicable. Quantitative triangulation via revenue multiples, margin analysis, or DCF-derived benchmarks cannot be reliably executed without disclosed financials for Baykar or sufficient comparable-stage peers. Public market data from DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS exists but may misrepresent the operational and customer concentration profile of a domestic defense integrator.

The rumored $10B valuation sits at the intersection of Stage uncertainty and sector illiquidity. Without audited financials, comparable revenue or EBITDA figures, or a denser peer set at similar funding maturity, a supported valuation range cannot be quantitatively derived. Key uncertainties include undisclosed total capital raised, export-market dependency, and program concentration risk—factors material to Defense & Security valuations but absent from available data.
